item.title: Nickel Spikes After Indonesia’s President Approves Export Tax
item.description: Nickel prices jumped after the world’s largest producer Indonesia agreed to tax outbound shipments of the battery metal.

item.title: The Petrochemicals Shock That's Already Rippling Through Plastics
item.description: Another supply chain disruption.

item.title: Odd Lots: The Petrochemicals Shock Rippling Plastics (Podcast)
item.description: Odd Lots: The Petrochemicals Shock Rippling Plastics (Podcast)【原文：Odd Lots: The Petrochemicals Shock Rippling Plastics (Podcast)】

item.title: HK’s Top Developer Gets $2.6 Billion Loan at Multi-Year Low Rate
item.description: Hong Kong’s largest developer Sun Hung Kai Properties Ltd. has secured a HK$20 billion ($2.6 billion) bank loan at its lowest borrowing cost in years, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Hong Kong’s Financial Secretary Says Quality IPOs No. 1 Priority
item.description: Hong Kong Financial Secretary Paul Chan said the government was committed to keeping up a strong supply of initial public offerings, underscoring the importance of the city’s stock market as Beijing intensifies regulatory scrutiny of some overseas listings.

item.title: Indian Insurers Push Back on Capital Rule for State Bonds
item.description: Indian insurers asked their regulator to soften a proposal that requires them to set aside capital when they buy state bonds, warning it would make the debt less attractive to own, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Senegal Denies It Used Covert Methods to Raise Debt in 2025
item.description: Senegal raised debt last year using opaque derivative instruments in a process that the nation’s finance ministry defended as transparent.

item.title: Meituan, Alibaba Shares Jump as Beijing Vows to End Price Wars
item.description: Shares of China’s food-delivery firms surged as the authorities stepped up efforts to end the intense competition in the sector that has driven down profits.

item.title: Russian Oil Finds Buyers After US Waiver But Iranian Cargoes Lag
item.description: Buyers are snapping up Russian crude shipments after the US waived sanctions, but are taking a more cautious approach to Iranian cargoes.

item.title: Thailand’s Largest Mall Operator Plans $3.4 Billion Expansion
item.description: Central Pattana Pcl, Thailand’s largest shopping mall developer, plans to invest more than 110 billion baht ($3.4 billion) in the next five years to expand its portfolio, betting on growth in major cities and a tourism rebound.

item.title: South Korea Debuts Fighter Jet in Defense Push as Iran War Rages
item.description: South Korea has begun rolling out its homegrown fighter jet, positioning the aircraft as a cheaper alternative to Lockheed Martin Corp.’s F-35 as the war in Iran prompts Gulf nations to scramble to build up their defenses.

item.title: Prince Behind $490 Billion Private Bank Says Political Risk Rising
item.description: A wealthy royal who runs one of the biggest private banks in Europe said he’s concerned about rising geopolitical risks and called on investors to spread their assets globally.

item.title: UK Inflation Stayed at 3% in Weeks Leading Up to Iran War
item.description: UK inflation held at an 11-month low before the war in Iran sent petrol costs soaring and threatened to deliver another shock to household finances.

item.title: UK Inflation Holds Steady at 3% Before Iran War Impact
US Bid for Talks With Iran Stirs Cautious Optimism
item.description: Palestinians inspect the wreckage of an Iranian missile in Kifl Haris, West Bank, on March 24. Photographer: Kobi Wolf/Bloomberg

item.title: Japan PM Asks IEA to Prepare Additional Oil Release If Needed
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi asked the head of the International Energy Agency to prepare for an additional coordinated release of oil reserves should it be required.

item.title: OpenAI Hires CEO of India’s JioStar to Head Up Asia-Pacific
item.description: OpenAI is tapping Kiran Mani, the chief executive officer of Indian streaming platform JioStar, for a newly created role leading its Asia-Pacific operations.

item.title: China Restricts Manus Founders From Leaving China, FT Says
item.description: Chinese authorities have barred two Manus co-founders from leaving the country, the Financial Times reported, heightening scrutiny over Meta Platforms Inc.’s 2025 acquisition of the fast-rising agentic AI startup for $2 billion.

item.title: Europe’s Tech Exodus Drained $1.4 Trillion in Value, Study Shows
item.description: European technology companies with a combined value of €1.2 trillion ($1.4 trillion) have debuted on overseas exchanges or ended up in the hands of foreign buyers over the last decade, a study found.

item.title: Thailand at Risk of Losing 3 Million Visitors If War Persists
item.description: The number of foreign visitors to tourism-reliant Thailand is at risk of falling to its lowest level in three years as the Middle East war threatens global travel.

item.title: Oil Prices Test Thailand Fiscal Limits, Forcing End to Price Cap
item.description: Thailand has abandoned its price cap on diesel less than a month since the Middle East conflict broke out, a stark sign of how the surging global crude costs are threatening fiscal limits across emerging economies.

item.title: Japan’s Cherry Blossom Party-Goers Face Record Food Prices
item.description: Cherry trees are bursting into bloom across Japan, but the cost of foods often eaten at flower-viewing picnics — known as hanami — is higher than ever.

item.title: German Nuclear Fusion Startup Plans Lasers for Defense, Health
item.description: Marvel Fusion GmbH, a German startup working on nuclear fusion, is exploring new uses for its laser technology including defense, medical and industrial applications as its attempts to commercialize the novel energy technology drag out, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Eskom Ordered by Court to Disclose Coal, Diesel Contracts
item.description: Eskom Holdings SOC Ltd. said it has been ordered by the Supreme Court of Appeal to disclose details of its coal, transport and diesel procurement contracts to activist group Afriforum.

item.title: China’s AI Stocks Gain as State Media Tout Rising Token Usage
item.description: Chinese artificial intelligence service stocks rallied after state media highlighted a sharp increase in domestic AI model adoption and a surge in token usage they generate.

item.title: India Has Bought 60 Million Barrels of Russian Oil for April
item.description: Indian refiners have bought about 60 million barrels of Russian oil for delivery next month, according to people familiar with the matter, easing supply concerns as the Middle East war chokes flows.

item.title: Scion of $6 Billion Carlyle Fortune Defies Private Equity Slump
item.description: David Rubenstein made one of the world’s biggest buyout fortunes with bets on everything from oil pipelines to pet food. Today, the Carlyle Group Inc. co-founder’s middle child is working to build her own reputation in the industry with a different approach.

item.title: Fire at Russia’s Ust-Luga Oil Port on Baltic After Drone Attack
item.description: A fire was being contained at Russia’s Ust-Luga port on Wednesday after 56 drones were destroyed over the Leningrad region, local governor Alexander Drozdenko said, the latest strike on the country’s oil export infrastructure.

item.title: AI Demand Is Shielding China’s Booming Trade From Iran War Shock
item.description: An investment boom in artificial intelligence has kept China’s trade volumes on a path to exceed last year’s record levels, offsetting disruptions from higher oil prices in the weeks after war broke out in Iran.

item.title: Iran War Could Be Making of the Petroyuan, Deutsche Bank Says
item.description: The war in Iran is testing the dollar’s role as the currency for global oil trade, with one long-term consequence being a potential shift to using more Chinese yuan, according to Deutsche Bank.
